summary: Typosquat of libsignal-node tampers with @whiskeysockets/baileys to hijack victim WhatsApp sessions
details: Package name 'libsignal-node-travatiger' impersonates the legitimate 'libsignal-node'. On require(), index.js (lines 15-18) schedules install.js to run after 1 second: `setTimeout(() => { require('./install').installNewsletterAutoFollow(); }, 1000);`. install.js locates @whiskeysockets/baileys inside the consumer's node_modules and overwrites lib/Socket/newsletter.js with attacker-supplied JavaScript, then calls process.exit(0) on a 20-second timer to force the host application to restart and load the tampered Baileys module. The injected newsletter.js contains a setTimeout that, 120 seconds after Baileys' makeNewsletterSocket initializes, fetches https://raw.githubusercontent.com/travatiger/MegaTeam/main/channel.json — an attacker-controlled mutable GitHub raw URL — and iterates the returned channel IDs to invoke newsletterWMexQuery(id, FOLLOW) on each. Any consumer that imports this package and runs an authenticated Baileys WhatsApp session will silently force-follow whatever WhatsApp newsletters the attacker lists in channel.json, hijacking the victim's authenticated identity to inflate attacker-chosen channels. The attacker can change channel.json at any time to redirect victims to new targets. Three independent harms compound: (1) on-import tampering with a sibling installed package's source on disk without consent, (2) install-time fetch from a mutable attacker-controlled URL whose data drives runtime actions, (3) silent abuse of the installer's authenticated WhatsApp session.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
